title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Statistical models rely assumptions valid inference. Violations assumptions can lead biased estimates, incorrect standard errors, misleading conclusions. modeldiag package provides unified framework diagnosing assumptions across multiple model classes, including: Linear models Logistic regression Count models (Poisson) Survival models (Cox proportional hazards) vignette introduces statistical intuition behind common diagnostics implement using modeldiag.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Consider classical linear regression model: Y=Xβ+ε,ε∼N(0,σ2I)Y = X\beta + \varepsilon, \quad \varepsilon \sim N(0, \sigma^2 ) Valid inference depends several assumptions error term ε\varepsilon.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Multicollinearity occurs predictors highly correlated. inflates variance coefficient estimates. Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) defined : VIFj=11−Rj2\text{VIF}_j = \frac{1}{1 - R_j^2} Rj2R_j^2 obtained regressing predictor XjX_j predictors. Large VIF values indicate unstable estimates.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Heteroscedasticity occurs : Var(εi)≠σ2 \text{Var}(\varepsilon_i) \neq \sigma^2 Breusch–Pagan test evaluates whether residual variance depends predictors.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Autocorrelation arises : Cov(εi,εj)≠0\text{Cov}(\varepsilon_i, \varepsilon_j) \neq 0 Durbin–Watson statistic tests first-order autocorrelation.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Many inferential procedures assume: ε∼N(0,σ2) \varepsilon \sim N(0, \sigma^2) Shapiro–Wilk test evaluates assumption.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Influential points disproportionately affect model estimates. Cook’s distance measures influence: Di=(β̂−β̂*())TXTX(β̂−β̂*())pσ̂2 D_i = \frac{( \hat{\beta} - \hat{\beta}*{()} )^T X^T X (\hat{\beta} - \hat{\beta}*{()})}{p \hat{\sigma}^2}

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: can also run individual checks need one diagnostic.
code: model_lm <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = mtcars) diag_lm <- diagnose_model(model_lm) summary(diag_lm) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp     disp  #> 4.844618 2.736633 7.324517  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>         wt         hp       disp  #> "Moderate" "Moderate"     "High"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  heteroskedasticity  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.8143398  #> Residual variance appears approximately constant.  #>  #> --  autocorrelation  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.01611772  #> Residuals appear autocorrelated; consider time-series adjustments.  #>  #> --  linearity  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.000869965  #> Evidence against linearity in the model; consider adding nonlinear terms or transformations.  #>  #> --  normality  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.03304597  #> Residuals deviate from normality; check model assumptions.  #>  #> --  outliers  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  3  #> Influential observations:  Chrysler Imperial, Toyota Corolla, Maserati Bora  #> Cook's distances for influential observations: #> Chrysler Imperial    Toyota Corolla     Maserati Bora  #>         0.3199707         0.1529771         0.3402911  #> 3 influential observation(s) detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems. check_vif(model_lm) #> $success #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $vif #>       wt       hp     disp  #> 4.844618 2.736633 7.324517  #>  #> $note #> [1] "VIF severity by predictor: wt (Moderate), hp (Moderate), disp (High)" #>  #> $collinear_predictors #> character(0) #>  #> $severities #>         wt         hp       disp  #> "Moderate" "Moderate"     "High" check_heteroskedasticity(model_lm) #>  #>  studentized Breusch-Pagan test #>  #> data:  model #> BP = 0.9459, df = 3, p-value = 0.8143 check_autocorrelation(model_lm) #>  #>  Durbin-Watson test #>  #> data:  model #> DW = 1.3673, p-value = 0.01612 #> alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0 check_linearity(model_lm) #>  #>  RESET test #>  #> data:  model #> RESET = 14.01, df1 = 1, df2 = 27, p-value = 0.00087 check_normality(model_lm) #>  #>  Shapiro-Wilk normality test #>  #> data:  res #> W = 0.92734, p-value = 0.03305 check_outliers(model_lm) #> $cooks_distance #>           Mazda RX4       Mazda RX4 Wag          Datsun 710      Hornet 4 Drive  #>        1.152035e-02        4.621112e-03        1.598334e-02        1.283888e-04  #>   Hornet Sportabout             Valiant          Duster 360           Merc 240D  #>        1.839055e-03        1.560119e-02        1.053270e-02        1.313511e-02  #>            Merc 230            Merc 280           Merc 280C          Merc 450SE  #>        2.525382e-03        3.671067e-03        2.606104e-02        1.551454e-03  #>          Merc 450SL         Merc 450SLC  Cadillac Fleetwood Lincoln Continental  #>        1.049983e-04        5.648180e-03        7.218880e-05        1.298764e-02  #>   Chrysler Imperial            Fiat 128         Honda Civic      Toyota Corolla  #>        3.199707e-01        1.196019e-01        9.092102e-03        1.529771e-01  #>       Toyota Corona    Dodge Challenger         AMC Javelin          Camaro Z28  #>        2.215865e-02        4.218196e-02        4.909944e-02        7.181085e-03  #>    Pontiac Firebird           Fiat X1-9       Porsche 914-2        Lotus Europa  #>        6.980693e-02        4.163138e-04        1.732523e-06        5.959750e-02  #>      Ford Pantera L        Ferrari Dino       Maserati Bora          Volvo 142E  #>        7.279943e-03        1.100867e-02        3.402911e-01        8.796726e-03  #>  #> $influential_points #> Chrysler Imperial    Toyota Corolla     Maserati Bora  #>                17                20                31

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Logistic regression models probability: logit(P(Y=1))=Xβ \text{logit}(P(Y=1)) = X\beta

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: model assumes linear relationship predictors log-odds: log⁡(p1−p) \log\left(\frac{p}{1-p}\right) Box–Tidwell test evaluates assumption.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Hosmer–Lemeshow test compares observed expected counts across groups.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Complete quasi-complete separation occurs predictors perfectly classify outcomes, leading unstable infinite estimates.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Individual logistic regression diagnostics can called directly.
code: model_glm <- glm(am ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, family = binomial) diag_glm <- diagnose_model(model_glm) summary(diag_glm) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp  #> 2.444297 2.444297  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>         wt         hp  #> "Moderate" "Moderate"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  linearity_logit  -- #>  #>    MLE of lambda Score Statistic (t) Pr(>|t|)   #> wt     -0.088743              2.2318  0.03412 * #> hp      3.205811              0.8980  0.37711   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> iterations =  13  #>  #> Score test for null hypothesis that all lambdas = 1: #> F = 3.6654, df = 2 and 27, Pr(>F) = 0.03905 #>  #>  #> --  goodness_of_fit  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.6716511  #> Model fit appears adequate.  #>  #> --  influential_observations  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  2  #> Influential observations:  Mazda RX4 Wag, Toyota Corona  #> Cook's distances for influential observations: #> Mazda RX4 Wag Toyota Corona  #>     0.2034041     0.7917908  #> 2 influential observation(s) detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems.  #>  #> --  separation_issues  -- #>  #> No separation issues detected. check_box_tidwell(model_glm) #>    MLE of lambda Score Statistic (t) Pr(>|t|)   #> wt     -0.088743              2.2318  0.03412 * #> hp      3.205811              0.8980  0.37711   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> iterations =  13  #>  #> Score test for null hypothesis that all lambdas = 1: #> F = 3.6654, df = 2 and 27, Pr(>F) = 0.03905 check_hosmer_lemeshow(model_glm) #>  #>  Hosmer and Lemeshow goodness of fit (GOF) test #>  #> data:  model$y, fitted(model) #> X-squared = 2.3502, df = 4, p-value = 0.6717 check_influential_glm(model_glm) #> $cooks_distance #>           Mazda RX4       Mazda RX4 Wag          Datsun 710      Hornet 4 Drive  #>        1.725110e-02        2.034041e-01        1.054039e-03        1.686756e-03  #>   Hornet Sportabout             Valiant          Duster 360           Merc 240D  #>        3.512351e-03        4.413520e-05        9.441132e-02        1.553660e-04  #>            Merc 230            Merc 280           Merc 280C          Merc 450SE  #>        1.805692e-03        1.692468e-04        1.692468e-04        9.043823e-07  #>          Merc 450SL         Merc 450SLC  Cadillac Fleetwood Lincoln Continental  #>        1.047495e-04        5.277112e-05        1.234078e-13        1.734386e-14  #>   Chrysler Imperial            Fiat 128         Honda Civic      Toyota Corolla  #>        1.619112e-13        1.309867e-03        7.429752e-07        7.402953e-06  #>       Toyota Corona    Dodge Challenger         AMC Javelin          Camaro Z28  #>        7.917908e-01        2.834875e-04        8.715763e-04        1.812379e-03  #>    Pontiac Firebird           Fiat X1-9       Porsche 914-2        Lotus Europa  #>        1.563880e-05        2.899997e-05        9.799693e-05        2.790544e-09  #>      Ford Pantera L        Ferrari Dino       Maserati Bora          Volvo 142E  #>        5.218144e-03        2.458477e-03        8.109529e-02        1.007256e-01  #>  #> $influential_points #> Mazda RX4 Wag Toyota Corona  #>             2 title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Poisson regression assumes: Y∼Poisson(λ),log⁡(λ)=Xβ Y \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda), \quad \log(\lambda) = X\beta

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: key assumption : Var(Y)=𝔼(Y) \text{Var}(Y) = \mathbb{E}(Y) Overdispersion occurs : Var(Y)>𝔼(Y) \text{Var}(Y) > \mathbb{E}(Y) leads underestimated standard errors.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Excess zeros beyond Poisson model predicts may indicate zero-inflated process.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Individual Poisson diagnostics available targeted checks.
code: model_pois <- glm(carb ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, family = poisson) diag_pois <- diagnose_model(model_pois) summary(diag_pois) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp  #> 1.401553 1.401553  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>           wt           hp  #> "Negligible" "Negligible"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  overdispersion  -- #>  #> Residual deviance:  12.27804  #> Degrees of freedom:  29  #> Ratio:  0.4233806  #> [OK] No evidence of overdispersion. #>  #> --  zero_inflation  -- #>  #> Observed zeros:  0  ( 0 %) #> Expected zeros:  3  ( 9.3 %) #> [OK] No evidence of zero-inflation. #>  #> --  influential_observations  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  0  #> No observations exceed the influence threshold. #> No influential observations detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems.  #>  #> --  residual_analysis  -- #>  #> Mean residual:  -0.04200633  #> SD residual:  0.6278888  #> Min residual:  -0.8656074  #> Max residual:  1.497036 check_overdispersion(model_pois) #> $residual_deviance #> [1] 12.27804 #>  #> $df #> [1] 29 #>  #> $ratio #> [1] 0.4233806 #>  #> $overdispersed #> [1] FALSE check_zero_inflation(model_pois) #> $observed_zeros #> [1] 0 #>  #> $expected_zeros #> [1] 2.978489 #>  #> $prop_observed #> [1] 0 #>  #> $prop_expected #> [1] 0.09307777 #>  #> $zero_inflated #> [1] FALSE check_residual_analysis(model_pois) #> $mean_residual #> [1] -0.04200633 #>  #> $sd_residual #> [1] 0.6278888 #>  #> $min_residual #> [1] -0.8656074 #>  #> $max_residual #> [1] 1.497036

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Cox proportional hazards model assumes: h(t|X)=h0(t)exp⁡(Xβ) h(t | X) = h_0(t) \exp(X\beta)

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: key assumption hazard ratios constant time. Schoenfeld residuals used test: ∂β(t)∂t=0 \frac{\partial \beta(t)}{\partial t} = 0

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Individual Cox model diagnostics can also run directly.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Diagnostic plots help identify violations visually.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: modeldiag package provides unified extensible framework model diagnostics, combining statistical rigor practical usability. integrating multiple diagnostic tools consistent interface, simplifies process validating model assumptions across diverse modeling frameworks.

title: Introduction to modeldiag
text: Cook, R. D., & Weisberg, S. (1982). Residuals Influence Regression. Chapman & Hall. Breusch, T. S., & Pagan, . R. (1979). Simple Test Heteroscedasticity Random Coefficient Variation. Econometrica. Durbin, J., & Watson, G. S. (1950, 1951). Testing Serial Correlation Least Squares Regression. Biometrika. Shapiro, S. S., & Wilk, M. B. (1965). Analysis Variance Test Normality. Biometrika. Hosmer, D. W., Lemeshow, S., & Sturdivant, R. X. (2013). Applied Logistic Regression. Wiley. Cox, D. R. (1972). Regression Models Life-Tables. JRSS. Cameron, . C., & Trivedi, P. K. (2013). Regression Analysis Count Data. Cambridge University Press.

code: @Manual{,   title = {modeldiag: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models},   author = {Emmanuel Adewuyi and Adewale Lukman and Abiola Owolabi},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.1},   url = {https://github.com/Teniola17/modeldiag}, }

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: goal modeldiag provide comprehensive diagnostic checks statistical models including linear models, generalized linear models, survival models.

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: can install development version modeldiag GitHub :

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: basic example shows diagnose linear model:
code: library(modeldiag)  # Fit a linear model model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = mtcars)  # Run diagnostics diagnostics <- diagnose_model(model)  # View summary summary(diagnostics)  # Plot diagnostics plot(diagnostics)

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: package currently supports: Linear models (lm): Tests multicollinearity, heteroscedasticity, autocorrelation, normality, outliers Binomial family: Tests linearity logit, goodness fit, influential observations, separation Poisson family: Tests overdispersion, zero-inflation, residual analysis Cox proportional hazards models (coxph): Tests proportional hazards assumption, influential observations, functional form

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: model type specific diagnostic tests:

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) multicollinearity Breusch-Pagan test heteroscedasticity Durbin-Watson test autocorrelation Shapiro-Wilk test normality residuals Cook’s distance influential observations

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: VIF multicollinearity Box-Tidwell test linearity logit Hosmer-Lemeshow test goodness fit Complete/quasi-complete separation detection Cook’s distance influential observations

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: VIF multicollinearity Overdispersion test Zero-inflation test Residual analysis Cook’s distance influential observations

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: VIF multicollinearity (warnings) Schoenfeld residuals test proportional hazards dfbetas influential observations Guidance functional form assessment

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: plot() method provides model-specific diagnostic plots: Residuals vs Fitted values Q-Q plots Scale-Location plots Cook’s distance plots ACF plots time series residuals (linear models) Schoenfeld residual plots (Cox models) dfbeta plots (Cox models) Martingale residual plots (Cox models)

title: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models
text: encounter bug, please file issue minimal reproducible example GitHub.#

title: Check Autocorrelation — check_autocorrelation
code: model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars) check_autocorrelation(model) #>  #> 	Durbin-Watson test #>  #> data:  model #> DW = 1.3624, p-value = 0.02061 #> alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0 #>

title: Diagnose Statistical Models — diagnose_model.glm

title: Diagnose Statistical Models — diagnose_model.glm
code: # S3 method for class 'glm' diagnose_model(model, ...)  # S3 method for class 'lm' diagnose_model(model, ...)  # S3 method for class 'coxph' diagnose_model(model, ...)  diagnose_model(model, ...)

text: model fitted model object. ... Additional arguments passed specific methods.

title: Diagnose Statistical Models — diagnose_model.glm
text: object class "model_diagnostics" containing results various diagnostic tests.

title: Diagnose Statistical Models — diagnose_model.glm
code: # Linear model diagnostics model_lm <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars) diag_lm <- diagnose_model(model_lm) summary(diag_lm) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp  #> 1.766625 1.766625  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>           wt           hp  #> "Negligible" "Negligible"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  heteroskedasticity  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.6438038  #> Residual variance appears approximately constant.  #>  #> --  autocorrelation  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.02061255  #> Residuals appear autocorrelated; consider time-series adjustments.  #>  #> --  linearity  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.0007226153  #> Evidence against linearity in the model; consider adding nonlinear terms or transformations.  #>  #> --  normality  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.03427476  #> Residuals deviate from normality; check model assumptions.  #>  #> --  outliers  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  4  #> Influential observations:  Chrysler Imperial, Fiat 128, Toyota Corolla, Maserati Bora  #> Cook's distances for influential observations: #> Chrysler Imperial          Fiat 128    Toyota Corolla     Maserati Bora  #>         0.4236109         0.1574263         0.2083933         0.2720397  #> 4 influential observation(s) detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems.  #>  plot(diag_lm)   # Logistic regression diagnostics model_glm <- glm(am ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, family = binomial) diag_glm <- diagnose_model(model_glm) summary(diag_glm) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp  #> 2.444297 2.444297  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>         wt         hp  #> "Moderate" "Moderate"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  linearity_logit  -- #>  #>    MLE of lambda Score Statistic (t) Pr(>|t|)   #> wt     -0.088743              2.2318  0.03412 * #> hp      3.205811              0.8980  0.37711   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> iterations =  13  #>  #> Score test for null hypothesis that all lambdas = 1: #> F = 3.6654, df = 2 and 27, Pr(>F) = 0.03905 #>  #>  #> --  goodness_of_fit  -- #>  #> p-value:  0.6716511  #> Model fit appears adequate.  #>  #> --  influential_observations  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  2  #> Influential observations:  Mazda RX4 Wag, Toyota Corona  #> Cook's distances for influential observations: #> Mazda RX4 Wag Toyota Corona  #>     0.2034041     0.7917908  #> 2 influential observation(s) detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems.  #>  #> --  separation_issues  -- #>  #> No separation issues detected.  #>   # Poisson regression diagnostics model_pois <- glm(carb ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, family = poisson) diag_pois <- diagnose_model(model_pois) summary(diag_pois) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> -- Variance inflation factors  #>  #>       wt       hp  #> 1.401553 1.401553  #> -- VIF severity by predictor:  #>  #>           wt           hp  #> "Negligible" "Negligible"  #> -- Severity legend:  #> * < 2 : Negligible #> * 2 - 5 : Moderate #> * 5 - 10 : High #> * >= 10 : Severe #>  #> --  overdispersion  -- #>  #> Residual deviance:  12.27804  #> Degrees of freedom:  29  #> Ratio:  0.4233806  #> [OK] No evidence of overdispersion. #>  #> --  zero_inflation  -- #>  #> Observed zeros:  0  ( 0 %) #> Expected zeros:  3  ( 9.3 %) #> [OK] No evidence of zero-inflation. #>  #> --  influential_observations  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  0  #> No observations exceed the influence threshold. #> No influential observations detected. Influential observations have Cook's distance above 4/n. Review these cases for leverage, data entry issues, or model fit problems.  #>  #> --  residual_analysis  -- #>  #> Mean residual:  -0.04200633  #> SD residual:  0.6278888  #> Min residual:  -0.8656074  #> Max residual:  1.497036  #>   # Cox proportional hazards diagnostics library(survival) data(lung) #> Warning: data set 'lung' not found model_cox <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ age + sex + ph.ecog, data = lung) diag_cox <- diagnose_model(model_cox) summary(diag_cox) #> -- Model Diagnostics Summary --------------------------------------------------- #>  #> --  multicollinearity  -- #>  #> VIF not applicable to Cox models (no intercept).  #>  #> --  proportional_hazards  -- #>  #> Global test p-value:  0.2155549  #>             chisq df         p #> age     0.1879877  1 0.6645968 #> sex     2.3054372  1 0.1289220 #> ph.ecog 2.0542488  1 0.1517821 #> GLOBAL  4.4636576  3 0.2155549 #> Proportional hazards assumption appears reasonable.  #>  #> --  influential_observations  -- #>  #> Number of influential points:  10  #> Influential observations:  3, 6, 17, 32, 36, 37, 70, 84, 88, 128  #> Max |dfbeta| for influential observations: #>         3         6        18        33        37        38        71        85  #> 0.2103167 0.4334764 0.3724475 0.2236608 0.5149764 0.2628997 0.2410623 0.4614677  #>        89       129  #> 0.2431150 0.2057780  #> Influential observations have |dfbeta| > 0.2 for at least one coefficient. Review these cases. #>  #> --  functional_form  -- #>  #> Functional form check: Review plots of martingale residuals vs predictors for nonlinearity.  #>

what: modeldiag: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models — modeldiag-package
title: modeldiag: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models — modeldiag-package
text: Provides unified framework diagnosing common issues statistical models including linear models, generalized linear models (logistic Poisson regression), survival models. Implements tests multicollinearity, heteroscedasticity, autocorrelation, normality, influential observations, overdispersion, zero-inflation, proportional hazards assumptions. Includes visualization methods graphical diagnostics. Methods based established approaches including Fox Monette (1992) doi:10.1080/01621459.1992.10475190 , Breusch Pagan (1979) doi:10.2307/1911963 , Dean Lawless (1989) doi:10.1080/01621459.1989.10478792 .

title: modeldiag: Comprehensive Diagnostics for Statistical Models — modeldiag-package
text: Maintainer: Emmanuel Adewuyi emmanuel.adewuyi@lshtm.ac.uk Authors: Adewale Lukman Abiola Owolabi

title: modeldiag 0.1.1
text: Improved multicollinearity reporting summary() linear model diagnostics. Fixed Authors@R metadata package builds derive Author Maintainer correctly.

title: modeldiag 0.1.2
text: Severity mapping: < 2 = Negligible; 2–5 = Moderate; 5–10 = High; >= 10 = Severe. check_vif() now returns severities collinear_predictors (predictors VIF >= 10). summary() prints per-predictor severities severity legend. Tests updated cover severity labels collinear predictor listing. diagnose_model.lm() now includes linearity test suite. summary output interprets whether evidence linearity.

title: modeldiag 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-05-28 Initial CRAN submission. Linear models (lm) Generalized linear models (glm) binomial poisson families Cox proportional hazards models (coxph) Multicollinearity (VIF) Heteroscedasticity (Breusch-Pagan test) Autocorrelation (Durbin-Watson test) Normality (Shapiro-Wilk test) Influential observations (Cook’s distance, dfbetas) Overdispersion (Poisson models) Zero-inflation (Poisson models) Proportional hazards (Cox models) Linearity logit (Logistic models) Goodness fit (Hosmer-Lemeshow test) Added print(), summary(), plot() methods diagnostic results Comprehensive plotting capabilities visual diagnostics Full documentation roxygen2 Unit tests testthat
